Title: Ted’s Golden Stone
Body (HTML):
Ted's Golden Stone delivers exceptional realism as a stonefly nymph pattern that exploits the most vulnerable stage of the stonefly lifecycle. The nymph stage represents the primary feeding opportunity for trout as these insects crawl along the bottom toward emergence, making this pattern far more effective than adult imitations that remain less available due to their aerial habits.

The realistic construction triggers aggressive responses from trout actively hunting bottom-dwelling nymphs. The proven effectiveness as a searching pattern makes this an essential choice for prospecting unfamiliar water where stonefly nymphs form a significant portion of available forage. When targeting trout feeding on natural stonefly nymphs, this pattern's superior realism and proven effectiveness deliver consistent success.

The distinctive half-and-half design creates a realistic forage silhouette that triggers aggressive strikes from tarpon, jacks, trevally, and snook—species that demand authentic presentation in clear water conditions.

Slater's refined approach combines proven baitfish imitation with superior proportions that outperform generic alternatives when targeting technically demanding fish. The pattern's distinctive profile and realistic coloration make it an essential choice for anglers pursuing trophy saltwater species where forage accuracy directly impacts success rates. Whether fishing flats or structure, this pattern delivers the authentic baitfish representation required to connect with selective predators.

Title: Slater's Poontastic
Body (HTML):
Slater's Poontastic in olive represents saltwater guru Ian Slater's refined approach to tarpon and multi-species saltwater fishing. Developed through extensive experience targeting selective flats species, this pattern delivers proven effectiveness across tarpon, permit, and other demanding saltwater predators.

The olive coloration matches natural forage in clear water conditions where color selection directly impacts success rates. Paired with the premium Gamakatsu SL12 hook—the choice of serious saltwater anglers—Slater's Poontastic combines proven pattern design with superior hardware that inspires confidence when pursuing trophy fish. Slater's expertise and this pattern's track record make it an essential choice for anglers targeting technical saltwater species where authenticity and quality materials determine success.

Title: Mara-Changer
Body (HTML): This articulated marabou fly delivers the lifelike baitfish profile serious saltwater anglers demand. The stacked marabou construction creates fluid, natural movement that provokes strikes across inshore flats and light offshore applications. Built on the dependable Gamakatsu SL12s hook, it combines superior hook geometry with the durability needed for demanding saltwater conditions. An essential pattern for fly fishers targeting multiple species with a single, reliable choice.

Title: Jig Sculpin Game Changer
Body (HTML):
The Game Changer is an extremely lifelike and versatile baitfish pattern originally designed by fly tying and musky guide guru Blane Chocklett. These flies are tied on articulated spines so they have incredibly realistic and lifelike movement in the water. 

One of our local tiers (the very talented Jeff Thompson of Bait Bucket Flies) painstakingly does these Gamechanger flies for us and his attention to detail is exceptional. These ones are on jig hooks with DB eyes which makes them a better choice for flowing water. Their sculpin profile makes them a perfect pattern for any predatory fish such as bull trout, tiger trout, lake trout, pike, musky, walleye and more.

Title: Millhouse Tarpon Toad
Body (HTML):
Millhouse Tarpon Toad represents Andy Mill's refined approach to targeting trophy tarpon in ultra-clear water conditions. Drawing on decades of elite tarpon expertise, Mill developed this pattern specifically for selective fish that demand authentic presentation and superior materials.

Dressed on the legendary Gamakatsu SL12 hook—Mill's hook of choice for serious tarpon work—this pattern combines proven design with premium hardware that withstands the explosive power of large tarpon. The toad profile delivers realistic silhouette and movement that connects with cautious fish in gin-clear flats where standard patterns fail. When pursuing trophy tarpon in technical conditions, Mill's proven patterns backed by world-class expertise provide the confidence and performance required for consistent success.

Title: Slaters Kiss Kwan
Body (HTML):
The Slaters Kiss Kwan bonefish pattern has been tried and tested on some of the most spooky bones on the planet - will brilliant success!

The fly is tied on the super strong Tiemco 811 hook. Designed by Fulling Mill consultant Ian Slater.

Title: Flaming Lamborghini
Body (HTML): This GT pattern excels at imitating coral grouper behavior near reef structures, making it an indispensable choice for serious saltwater anglers. Engineered with optimal weight distribution, it casts effortlessly to distant targets while maintaining the structural integrity needed to withstand aggressive strikes. Constructed on the proven Gamakatsu SL12 hook, this fly combines proven design principles with reliable materials for consistent performance in demanding conditions.

Title: Rainy's Flies - Tube Popper
Body (HTML):
Rainy's Flies Tube Popper represents a specialized bluewater pattern engineered for open ocean predators and trophy saltwater gamefish. The tube fly construction provides superior durability and allows quick leader changes without sacrificing the pattern itself—essential when targeting powerful fish capable of destroying conventional flies.

The popper design generates effective surface disturbance and attracts aggressive strikes from pelagic species through realistic baitfish imitation and commotion. Built for the demands of serious bluewater hunting, this pattern delivers the reliability and performance required when pursuing large saltwater gamefish in challenging offshore environments where equipment failure proves costly.

Title: Rainy's Flies - CB Wonky Pop
Body (HTML):
Rainy's Flies CB Wonky Pop features an articulated body design that delivers exaggerated movement and erratic action in the water. The segmented construction creates lifelike swimming motion that mimics wounded or fleeing baitfish, triggering predatory instincts in aggressive gamefish.

This popper's dynamic articulation generates compelling surface disturbance and unpredictable movement patterns that outperform rigid alternatives when fish demand realistic forage behavior. The articulated design proves particularly effective in clear water conditions where selective fish scrutinize prey presentation. Whether targeting pike, bass, or saltwater species, the CB Wonky Pop's distinctive movement profile delivers the exaggerated action necessary to provoke strikes from fish that ignore conventional popper presentations.

Title: Rainy's Flies - PSP Bubble-Head
Body (HTML): Rainy's Flies PSP Bubble-Head stands as a versatile big-game popper engineered for explosive topwater action across multiple predatory species. The signature hole through the head allows water passage, generating a distinctive fish-attracting racket and unique bubble trail that triggers aggressive strikes. This innovative design simultaneously reduces wind resistance during casting, improving accuracy and distance when targeting distant structure. The PSP Bubble-Head excels against pike, peacock bass, redfish, tarpon, and stripers—delivering consistent performance across freshwater and saltwater applications. The combination of proven popper mechanics with refined aerodynamics makes this pattern an efficient choice for anglers seeking reliable topwater results without sacrificing casting performance or fish-attracting capability.

Title: Rainy's Flies - Mini Wonk
Body (HTML): Rainy's Flies Mini Wonk delivers compact all-purpose popper performance for anglers targeting smaller predatory species and finicky fish. The diminutive profile excels when larger patterns prove counterproductive, offering realistic baitfish imitation in a refined package. This popper generates effective surface action without excessive disturbance, making it ideal for clear water conditions and selective fish. The Mini Wonk's versatility spans freshwater and saltwater applications, eliminating the need for multiple small-profile patterns. Whether targeting panfish, smaller bass, or juvenile saltwater species, this pattern combines proven popper mechanics with a scaled-down design that connects with fish when standard-sized alternatives fail to produce.

Title: Rainy's Flies - CB Taco Pescado
Body (HTML):
Rainy's Flies CB Taco Pescado functions as a versatile all-purpose popper pattern suited for diverse saltwater and freshwater applications. This proven design delivers reliable topwater action across multiple predatory species, from aggressive inshore hunters to open water gamefish. The popper's construction generates effective surface disturbance that attracts strikes through realistic baitfish imitation and commotion.

Its adaptability makes it an efficient choice for anglers targeting variable species without requiring extensive pattern inventories. Whether fishing flats, structure, or open water, the CB Taco Pescado's straightforward effectiveness and proven track record establish it as a dependable option for anglers seeking consistent topwater performance across diverse fishing scenarios.

Title: Rainy's Flies - CB Poppin' Feather-Head
Body (HTML):
Rainy's Flies CB Poppin' Feather-Head delivers specialized bluewater performance for sailfish and billfish applications. The distinctive feather-head construction creates realistic baitfish imitation while generating surface disturbance that attracts aggressive strikes from pelagic predators. This pattern excels in open ocean conditions where explosive topwater action triggers feeding responses in trophy-class fish.

The proven design combines proven materials with refined proportions, ensuring consistent performance across varying bluewater scenarios. Whether targeting sailfish on the flats or billfish in deeper waters, this pattern's reputation among experienced saltwater anglers makes it an essential choice for serious offshore hunters pursuing the ocean's most powerful gamefish.

Title: Crease Fly
Body (HTML):
Crease Fly functions as a versatile popper pattern equally effective in freshwater and saltwater applications against aggressive predatory species. Rather than producing deep popping disturbance, this pattern excels at imitating a skittering, fleeing baitfish—creating subtle surface commotion that triggers strikes from feeding fish.

The skipping and skittering action proves particularly deadly when fish are actively busting baitballs, where realistic escape behavior outperforms aggressive noise-making presentations. 

This all-purpose design eliminates the need for multiple patterns, making it an efficient choice for anglers targeting everything from largemouth bass to stripers to saltwater species. The refined surface disturbance mimics genuine forage behavior, connecting with fish in high-activity feeding situations where authenticity matters most.

Title: Yewchuk's 2 Timing Toad
Body (HTML): Yewchuk's 2 Timing Toad delivers high-floating frog imitation engineered for explosive topwater action. The Double Barrel head construction provides superior buoyancy and durability, keeping the pattern riding high in the surface film where predatory fish hunt. Realistic frog legs create lifelike movement and silhouette, triggering aggressive strikes from bass, pike, and other topwater hunters. This pattern excels in shallow vegetation and around structure where frogs naturally congregate, offering an authentic presentation that outperforms generic alternatives. The combination of proven head design and anatomically correct leg articulation makes this toad an essential choice for anglers targeting frog-feeding species in shallow water environments.

Title: Nyap 6/0
Body (HTML): Not Your Average Popper (NYAP) represents the gold standard in saltwater foam patterns, designed by Fulling Mill consultant James Christmas—a seasoned Alphonse Island guide with proven expertise in trophy GT fishing. This deceptively simple foam design has accounted for massive GTs across the world's premier flats destinations. Built on the legendary Gamakatsu SL12 hook, the NYAP delivers the durability and reliability demanded by explosive saltwater strikes. Quality matters here—inferior alternatives risk losing the fish of a lifetime when a GT's violent take destroys the foam body. Stock multiple copies in your box; experienced anglers know that one explosive hit often renders a pattern unusable, requiring backups for sustained success. The NYAP's proven track record makes it non-negotiable for serious GT hunters.

Title: Blackhurst Tactical CDC Caddis
Body (HTML): Blackhurst Tactical CDC Caddis exploits the most lethal moment in a caddis lifecycle—when emerging insects break free from their shuck and struggle to dry their wings in the film. Fish recognize this vulnerability instantly, making this pattern devastatingly effective even against the most selective trout. CDC material delivers precisely calibrated buoyancy to suspend the fly in the film where naturals congregate, presenting an irresistible target. Built on our tactical barbless hook—ultra-sharp, exceptionally strong, and finished in black nickel for maximum stealth—this pattern combines proven imitation with premium hardware. Tied in tan to match the most common emerging caddis, it's an essential addition for anglers targeting technical water where presentation and materials matter.

Title: Hickey’s Tungsten Bead Auto Emerger
Body (HTML): Hickey's Tungsten Bead Auto Emerger harnesses tungsten's superior density to achieve rapid depth penetration without resorting to split shot on your leader. The tungsten bead head efficiently drives this pattern into the lower water column where feeding fish congregate, making it ideal for searching deeper lies. Deploy it as your anchor fly in tandem rigs—pair it with a lighter nymph above or combine it with a lighter nymph and dry fly to systematically cover the entire water column in a single presentation. This versatile BWO emerger pattern excels in multi-fly setups, allowing you to fish multiple depths simultaneously and maximize your chances of connecting with selective trout across varying feeding zones.

Title: CDC Mayfly Emerger
Body (HTML): The RS2 stands as an essential tail water pattern that demands unconventional presentation tactics. While classified as an emerger, this fly achieves its greatest effectiveness when fished as a subsurface nymph rather than in traditional emerger fashion. Present it at the precise depth where fish are holing, delivering the fly directly into their feeding zone underwater. This counterintuitive approach unlocks the RS2's lethal potential, making it indispensable for anglers targeting selective trout in technical tail water environments where standard emerger presentations fall short.

Title: RS2
Body (HTML): The RS2 stands as an essential tail water pattern that demands unconventional presentation tactics. While classified as an emerger, this fly achieves its greatest effectiveness when fished as a subsurface nymph rather than in traditional emerger fashion. Present it at the precise depth where fish are holding, delivering the fly directly into their feeding zone underwater. Title: WD-40
Body (HTML):
• Western staple for small BWO and midge nymphs and emergers with a proven track record of consistent results

• Versatile emerger pattern that effectively imitates multiple insect species beyond its namesake applications

• Time-tested design that has earned its reputation through decades of field success across challenging waters

• Excels at the critical emerger stage when insects are most vulnerable to feeding trout

• Size selection is paramount—match your local hatch carefully for optimal effectiveness

• Delivers reliable performance in technical situations where selectivity demands precision

Title: CDC Emerging Caddis
Body (HTML):
• Targets caddis flies during their most vulnerable stage—breaking free from the shuck and drying their wings in the film

• Hangs perfectly in the water column where emerging insects are most exposed to feeding trout

• CDC material provides subtle buoyancy to keep the fly suspended in the top of the film where naturals congregate

• Proven effective by anglers who've tested this pattern extensively in the field

• Mimics the transitional phase when caddis are least able to escape, triggering aggressive strikes from selective fish

Title: Rocky Mountain Barr Emerger
Body (HTML): The Rocky Mountain Barr Emerger is an essential "guide-style" pattern designed to mimic a mayfly at its most vulnerable moment: just as it transitions from a nymph into a winged adult. This version features a distinct olive-brown tapered abdomen and a soft gray thorax, perfectly matching Blue-Winged Olive (BWO) hatches found throughout Western rivers and tailwaters. Meticulously handcrafted with a trailing shuck and split-wing profile, it creates an ultra-realistic silhouette that effectively fools selective trout feeding in or just below the surface film. Whether fished as a point fly in a tandem nymph rig or as a dropper behind a high-floating dry fly, this durable emerger is a reliable year-round performer for targeting picky fish.

Title: Parachute PMD Emerger
Body (HTML): The Parachute PMD Emerger is a must-have addition to any fly box, specifically designed to fool selective trout during critical Pale Morning Dun hatches. This meticulously handcrafted pattern features a low-riding profile that sits perfectly in the surface film, mimicking a vulnerable mayfly struggling to break through the meniscus. The vibrant yellow-orange abdomen is paired with a buoyant parachute hackle wrapped around a high-visibility post, ensuring you can track your drift even in choppy water or low-light conditions. Built on a premium barbless hook for superior sets and easy releases, this emerger is an essential "guide-style" fly that bridges the gap between a nymph and a full dry fly.

Title: Olive Sparkle Pupa Caddis Emerger
Body (HTML):
• Translucent Antron or Z-lon sheath encircles a dubbed olive underbody, replicating the shimmering gas bubbles caddis pupae use for ascent

• Deer hair wing and dark fur head enable effective presentation in the surface film or slightly subsurface during emergence

• Specifically designed for olive caddis hatches including the prolific Mother's Day Caddis emergence

• Versatile presentation options—dead-drift for subtle naturalism, twitch for movement simulation, or swing-and-lift to mimic rising naturals

• Sparkle effect triggers strikes from selective trout keyed on emerging caddis pupae during peak hatch periods

• Essential pattern for anglers pursuing consistent success during olive caddis emergences across prime trout waters

Title: PMD Biot Emerger
Body (HTML): The PMD Biot Emerger is a highly specialized fly pattern designed to imitate a Pale Morning Dun mayfly at its most vulnerable stage: when it is struggling to break through the surface film to transition from a nymph to an adult. This pattern is distinguished by its slender, segmented abdomen created with a turkey biot, which provides a realistic, tapered profile that accurately mimics the natural insect's body. To further enhance its realism, the fly often incorporates a trailing shuck made of antron or z-lon to represent a shedding nymphal skin, and a small, buoyant wing or wing case made of CDC, foam, or antron to keep the fly positioned correctly in the surface film. Primarily effective during the summer months when PMD hatches are most prolific, it is typically fished as a "dropper" behind a dry fly or dead-drifted to rising trout that are keying in on easy, low-riding prey.

Title: Gray Foam Wing Emerger
Body (HTML):
• Slender, low-riding profile imitates insects struggling through the surface film during critical emergence periods

• Buoyant foam wing provides high visibility while anchoring the fly in the strike zone where trout intercept vulnerable emergers

• Gray coloration precisely matches Blue-Winged Olives and Tricos in smaller sizes, with larger versions effectively imitating Pale Morning Dun emergences • Long-shank hook with gray superfine dubbing body and sparse microfiber tail deliver authentic emerging insect silhouette

• Foam construction maintains flotation in the surface film, though floatant application maximizes buoyancy and extends time in the feeding window

• Essential emerger pattern for matching selective trout during Blue-Winged Olive and Trico hatches across prime waters

Title: Kingfisher Butcher Trout Flies, Sea trout or Trout,
Body (HTML):
• Purpose: A highly effective attractor pattern used to trigger aggressive strikes from "lazy" fish, especially in rough or slightly colored water.
  • Imitation: Primarily excels as a fry or pin-fry imitator, but it can also represent various small aquatic insects.
  • Design: Typically features a flat silver body, oval silver rib, bright blue-black wings, and vibrant kingfisher blue or red accents (hackle and tail).
  • Target Species: A "killer" pattern for sea trout (especially at night) and both rainbow and brown trout in rivers, reservoirs, and loughs.
  • Usage: Best fished in the middle or on the point of a wet fly cast.

Title: Green Weenie Wet Fly

Fish it with confidence as a searching pattern when hatch activity remains uncertain or as a proven producer during established feeding periods.

Title: Bonefish Bitters
Body (HTML):

Title: Merkin Crab
Body (HTML): The Merkin Crab represents the gold standard in permit and bonefish patterns, designed by Del Brown—the angler with more permit on fly than anyone in history. This pattern accounts for the majority of his record-setting catches, earning its place as the definitive crab imitation for saltwater flats. Its proven effectiveness across permit, bonefish, and other shallow-water species makes it indispensable for serious saltwater anglers. Whether you're sight-casting to cruising permit or working bonefish flats, this fly delivers the realistic profile and movement that triggers strikes from the most selective fish. Essential for any permit angler's arsenal.

Title: Bean’s Orange Crush
Body (HTML): Mike Bean's Orange Crush was designed to be a visible pattern that is durable, catches fish, and has a different profile from other patterns that fish see day to day from anglers. The fly was also designed to be a pattern that can be skated or twitched to get a trout's attention. The Orange Crush imitates a Golden Stone that hatches on the South Fork of the Snake River in July and September. The fly also works when salmon flies or terrestrials are present and has proven itself on the Henry's Fork, Teton River, spring creeks, and mountain lakes in Idaho. The key to this fly is its high profile, durability, and effectiveness, and works best with some type of action put on it. The best way to fish it is to “twitch” or “skate” the fly when it is on the water. The more movement used with the fly the better. In faster water, dead drift the fly.

Title: Black Toast Ant
Body (HTML): The Black Toast Ant leverages exaggerated proportions to present an irresistible surface meal during flying ant hatches. Its pronounced silhouette provides the visual contrast and profile that trigger aggressive feeding responses from opportunistic trout. This pattern excels when ants are actively on the water, offering fish an easy target they cannot resist. Fish it with confidence during peak ant season for reliable surface action.

Title: Letort Hopper
Body (HTML): The Letort Hopper ranks among the most effective grasshopper imitations for consistent success on selective trout waters. This proven pattern delivers the realistic profile and natural presentation that discerning fish demand during terrestrial season. Whether drifted along grassy banks or cast to rising fish, the Letort's refined design triggers confident takes from trout keyed on natural grasshoppers. A trusted choice for anglers pursuing trophy fish when hoppers are on the menu.

Title: Chernobyl Ant
Body (HTML): The Hog Caller Hopper distinguishes itself through innovative hybrid rear legs—half foam, half rubber—that maintain superior flotation and upright positioning throughout the drift. This design refinement elevates performance beyond conventional foam hoppers, delivering the stability trout demand when terrestrials dominate the menu. The substantial profile and leggy silhouette make it equally effective as a high-visibility indicator or standalone terrestrial pattern during peak hopper season. Cast it confidently to banks, grass lines, and structure knowing the engineering delivers consistent results that anglers across the country have already validated.

Title: Barbless Mini Flopper
Body (HTML): The Barbless Mini Flopper punches above its weight as a compact foam bug that consistently produces results on summer waters. Its diminutive profile excels when fished tight to banks, beneath vegetation, and under overhanging trees where larger patterns spook cautious fish. The foam construction provides reliable flotation and creates subtle surface disturbance that triggers strikes from trout and bass in confined spaces. This pattern proves indispensable for precision casting into tight quarters where conventional bugs fail to deliver.

Title: Baile’s Panther Creek Hopper
Body (HTML): Angler's have a name for late summer, it's hopper season. Anyone who has tromped through a field on the way to a river and pushed a wall of grasshoppers in front of them knows the thrill of throwing one of these patterns to hungry and aggressive fish. This fly takes the standard hopper pattern and makes it just a little bit better; a foam body for all-day flotation, the deer hair wing-case stands tall, providing the unmistakable grasshopper profile, rubber legs for action, and an orange post for high visibility.

Title: Galactic Ant
Body (HTML): The Galactic Ant stands as a premier foam terrestrial pattern, combining synthetic and natural materials for unmatched performance on summer flats and grassy banks. Its foam body floats high throughout extended fishing sessions, while natural hackle delivers the delicate profile that suggests authentic movement to selective trout. This balance of buoyancy and lifelike presentation makes it essential when terrestrial insects dominate the menu. Whether ants are tumbling into the water or crawling along the banks, this pattern consistently converts rises from opportunistic feeders during peak terrestrial season.

Title: RIO's Brammer Seasoned Geezer
Body (HTML):
The Brammer Seasoned Geezer is a proven bass bug engineered for explosive topwater action. This pattern excels at drawing aggressive strikes from trophy bass in shallow water and around structure.

The carefully designed profile creates realistic movement and disturbance that mimics natural prey, while durable construction withstands repeated casts and hard-hitting takes. Whether you're working lily pads, fallen timber, or open flats, this fly delivers consistent results across varying conditions.

Strip it with authority or work it with subtle twitches to trigger predatory responses from even the most selective fish.

Title: RIO's My Oh My Dragonfly
Body (HTML): The My Oh My Dragonfly targets bass during peak feeding season from July through September when these predators aggressively pursue both nymphal and adult dragonflies. Tied on a trusted Gamakatsu B10S hook with premium Kahuna LT material for the abdomen, this pattern replicates the natural silhouette and movement that triggers savage strikes. Years of refinement ensure each design element performs exactly as bass expect. Fish it by casting and allowing a 7-10 second pause before twitching to animate the wings—strikes typically occur on initial landing or during the retrieve. Equally effective stripped off the surface or worked through subsurface zones.

Title: Slumpbuster Bouface Muddy Buddy Bunny Flies
Body (HTML): The Slumpbuster Bouface Muddy Buddy Bunny Fly delivers proven performance for anglers targeting trophy bass and trout across diverse water systems. This single fly combines cone head and bead head construction for superior casting distance and depth control, while its weighted design ensures rapid sinking and natural subsurface presentation. Premium materials replicate authentic prey profiles that trigger predatory responses in rivers, lakes, and streams. The durable construction withstands aggressive strikes and repeated casting, making it a reliable choice for varying current speeds and water clarity conditions. Strip through deep pools or work shallow flats with confidence.

Title: Swimming Frog Bass Fly Fishing Bug Wide Gape Bass Hook With Weed Guard
Body (HTML): The Swimming Frog pattern stands as a proven performer across multiple gamefish species, earning its reputation among serious anglers targeting smallmouth and largemouth bass throughout North America. Alaskan guides consistently rely on this design for salmon and trout, where the distinctive deer hair head creates substantial water disturbance that draws aggressive strikes from feeding fish. The wide gape bass hook paired with integrated weed guard ensures reliable hooksets while navigating vegetation and structure without snagging. This versatile bug excels in both shallow and moderate depths, making it an essential pattern for any predatory fish scenario. Whether you're a dedicated fly angler or seeking the perfect gift for someone who appreciates quality tackle, this proven design delivers results when it matters most.

Title: Bass Fly Fishing Flies - Surface Poppers Mouse Divers
Body (HTML): Hand-tied from premium deer hair, these Surface Poppers and Mouse Divers deliver authentic distress patterns that provoke explosive strikes from bass. The buoyant construction keeps flies riding high on the surface, creating the commotion and silhouette that trigger predatory responses. Each pattern mimics wounded insects or small rodents—the natural prey bass actively hunt. While engineered specifically for largemouth and smallmouth bass, their versatility extends to pike and trophy trout, making them valuable additions to any saltwater or freshwater predator angler's arsenal. The durable deer hair construction withstands repeated casts and aggressive takes, ensuring consistent performance throughout your fishing day.

Title: Brown Marabou Leech
Body (HTML): The Brown Marabou Leech capitalizes on leeches' status as a primary forage across lakes and moving water. Marabou feathers deliver the undulating motion that triggers predatory responses from trout, brook trout, and perch—mimicking a natural leech's vulnerable swimming behavior. The brown coloration proves particularly effective during overcast conditions when fish rely on silhouette and movement rather than precise color matching. A versatile pattern that works equally well stripped through stillwater or swung in current-fed environments.

Title: Fulling Mill Red Mirage Zebra Midge
Body (HTML): The Fulling Mill Red Mirage Zebra Midge employs a dual-bead system with tungsten weighting to penetrate quickly to feeding trout. Since midges form the foundation of trout diets across every water type, from fast freestones to spring-fed tailwaters, this pattern delivers year-round relevance. The reflective red body cuts through murky conditions while the tungsten bead ensures rapid descent into productive depths. An essential choice for anglers targeting selective trout feeding on nature's most abundant aquatic insect.

Title: Larson’s Legend Golden Stone Dry
Body (HTML): Larry Larsen's Legend emerged from competition-level refinement at the 2005 Jackson Hole One Fly event, solving a critical problem: large cutthroat approaching from below refuse high-floating or heavy patterns. The Legend rides lower and lighter than traditional stonefly imitations, allowing fish to inhale confidently. Equally effective as a nocturnal stone, Golden, Skwalla, or Salmon Fly pattern, it excels when twitched near structure in pressured water where selective trout demand precision. Fish it dry with a greased wing or deploy as a dropper—versatility that makes it invaluable for late-season and technical conditions.

Title: Bead Head Flymph - BWO
Body (HTML): The Bead Head Flymph—Blue Wing Olive revives the underutilized art of soft hackle swinging. This pattern shines during Blue Wing Olive emergences when trout haven't yet committed to surface feeding, intercepting fish in the critical transition zone. The bead head provides controlled depth while the soft hackle collar creates enticing movement across the current. Cast across and slightly downstream, then prepare for strikes as the fly begins its rise, a technique that delivers consistent results when conventional dry fly presentations fall short.

Title: Tarpon Toad Saltwater Fly
Body (HTML): The Tarpon Toad has earned its place in serious tarpon boxes through proven performance across multiple fisheries. Available in anchovy and mackerel patterns, it eliminates the need to carry multiple baitfish imitations while maintaining the realism and performance that discerning saltwater anglers expect.

Title: Tactical Slush Egg Jig
Body (HTML): The Tactical Slush Egg Jig delivers authentic presentation through carefully selected materials that achieve exceptional translucency when wet, creating a lifelike profile that appeals to selective fish. Built on a tactical jig hook paired with a tungsten bead, this pattern cuts through the water column efficiently, reaching target zones in demanding conditions. Title: Mickey Finn fishing fly
Body (HTML): The Mickey Finn stands as a testament to classic streamer design, evolving from the bucktail tradition into a northeastern staple. This iconic pattern excels when fished on the swing, its proven silhouette and color combination triggering aggressive strikes from trout and other gamefish. The Mickey Finn's enduring popularity reflects its effectiveness across varied water conditions, making it an essential addition to any serious angler's fly box. Title: Tungsten Cone Head Woolly Bugger
Body (HTML): The Tungsten Cone Head Woolly Bugger is purpose-built for reaching deep-holding predators in demanding conditions. Tungsten's superior density ensures rapid descent through heavy current and deep water columns where conventional patterns fall short. In still-water environments, pair this fly with a Depth Charge line to achieve maximum penetration; in fast-moving rivers, combine it with a Streamer Stripper for controlled depth management. The conehead design minimizes water resistance while maximizing sink rate, allowing you to maintain bottom contact and access trophy fish that refuse shallow presentations. An essential pattern for serious anglers targeting deep structure and aggressive species.

Title: Bonefish Gotcha Saltwater Fishing Flies
Body (HTML): The Bonefish Gotcha is the gold standard in saltwater bonefish imitations, earning universal recognition among serious flats anglers for its consistent ability to convert takes into hookups. Available in three distinct configurations engineered for specific depth zones and feeding conditions, this pattern adapts seamlessly to varying water columns and tidal stages. The Shallow version features plastic bead eyes for minimal sink rate in ultra-clear flats, the Tailing variant employs metal bead chain eyes for moderate descent during mid-water presentations, and the Deep configuration utilizes non-toxic metal dumbbell eyes for rapid penetration in deeper channels. Every accomplished bonefisherman maintains multiple Gotcha variations—a foundational pattern that belongs in every saltwater fly box.

Title: Puglisi Bluegill
Body (HTML): The Puglisi Bluegill targets a forage species that largemouth bass actively hunt yet many anglers overlook—bluegill fry represent a high-calorie meal that triggers aggressive feeding responses. This pattern excels as a strategic change-of-pace offering when standard presentations stall, particularly effective around shallow structure and vegetation. The weedless design allows confident presentation through dense cover where trophy bass ambush prey, eliminating snags that compromise casting efficiency. A sophisticated pattern that exploits a genuine feeding preference, making it essential for anglers pursuing selective, larger fish.

Title: Feather Changer
Body (HTML): The Feather Changer leverages natural feather construction to achieve exceptional castability without sacrificing movement or profile. Its highly articulated body generates lifelike swimming action that appeals to both freshwater and saltwater predators, while premium feather materials provide inherent motion even during dead-drift presentations. Available in proven color combinations optimized for diverse water conditions, this pattern performs equally well in rivers, lakes, and coastal environments. A versatile choice for anglers seeking a single streamer that delivers across multiple species and ecosystems.

Title: Hawkins Triple Double

Title: TeQueely Streamer

Title: Bead Head Flash Zonker

This pattern excels when targeting species that respond to bold profiles and dynamic movement—a proven choice for serious trophy hunters.

A proven performer that justifies its place in any serious bass angler's streamer collection.

Title: Chewing Gum Cased Caddis Barbless

Title: Grumpy Frumpy

Articulated buggy legs provide lifelike movement and tactile appeal, making this pattern an essential choice for targeting selective fish during warm-water months.

Title: Joe's Hopper Yellow Foam Body Grasshopper Flies
Body (HTML): • Improved version with yellow foam body. Originated in Michigan as the Michigan Hopper in the 1920s–1940s by Art Winnie, a Traverse City barber and fly tier who sold his flies commercially. It gained widespread fame in the 1950s when renowned angler and writer Joe Brooks praised and popularized it (including in his books), leading to its common name, Joe's Hopper. Though it has been somewhat overshadowed by modern foam-bodied hoppers, it remains a proven, durable classic for hopper season.

Title: Tungsten Bead Hot Spot Pheasant Tail Tactical Jig Chartreuse Czech Nymph Euro Fly
Body (HTML):
• Tungsten Bead Pheasant Tail Tactical Jig Czech Euro Nymph - A Euro Jigged version of the classic Pheasant Tail Nymph pattern created in the early 20th century by English fly tier Frank Sawyer; originally designed for chalk streams to imitate small mayfly nymphs, it became one of the most famous and effective subsurface flies in fly fishing history and a cornerstone of modern Euro nymphing
  • Heavy Tungsten Bead Jig Hook - Slotted tungsten bead head paired with 60-degree jig hook for rapid sink rate and snag-resistant, hook-point-up swimming action; perfect for Czech, French, Spanish, Polish, and tactical Euro nymphing in fast pocket water, deep runs, and riffles
  • Slim Pheasant Tail Profile - Ultra-realistic segmented body made from pheasant tail fibers with copper rib; slender design and natural movement imitate Baetis mayfly nymphs, midges, small stoneflies, and other key subsurface insects in clear, technical trout waters
  • Proven Year-Round Trout Killer - Legendary searching and match-the-hatch nymph that consistently produces brook, brown, rainbow, and cutthroat trout in rivers, tailwaters, and spring creeks; excels in Euro nymph rigs for drag-free drifts and deep, natural presentations.Premium Hand-Tied Quality - Expertly crafted Tungsten Bead Pheasant Tail Jig flies using durable premium materials and sharp barbless jig hooks; available in multiple sizes and variations to match seasonal hatches, water depths, and Euro nymphing conditions
